data.title: Amazon invests $5B in Anthropic as startup secures up to 5GW of Trainium chips
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) announced today it was investing $5B in Anthropic (ANTHRO) and plans to add up to $20B more to that figure in the years ahead.

Amazon shares were up 3% during early post-market trading on Monday.

On their end, Anthropic has committed to spending more than $100B over the next decade on Amazon Web Services tech. This includes current and future generations of Amazon's Trainium chip and tens of millions of Graviton cores. In addition, Anthropic will secure up to 5 GW of capacity to train and power their AI models. 

Amazon will invest $5B in Anthropic today and up to an additional $20B in the future, dependent on reaching certain commercial milestones. Amazon has previously invested $8B in Anthropic. 

The new partnership also provides AWS customers access to the full Anthropic-native Claude console from within AWS with no additional credentials, contracts, or billing relationships to manage. 

"Anthropic's commitment to run its large language models on AWS Trainium for the next decade reflects the progress we've made together on custom silicon, as we continue delivering the technology and infrastructure our customers need to build with generative AI," said Amazon CEO Andy Jassy.

"Our collaboration with Amazon will allow us to continue advancing AI research while delivering Claude to our customers, including the more than 100,000 building on AWS," said Anthropic CEO and co-founder Dario Amodei.

AWS will continue to serve as Anthropic's primary training and cloud provider.

data.title: Zions stock down as Q1 net interest income, margin miss estimates
data.description:
Zions Bancorporation (ZION) was trading lower despite an earnings beat in the first quarter as net interest income and margin fell short of expectations.

Shares were 1.81% lower to $61.91 Monday after market hours.

The regional lender reported a GAAP EPS of $1.56 for the first quarter of 2026, which compares with the year-ago figure of $1.13 and $1.76 in the fourth quarter of 2025. The consensus stands at $1.43.

Net interest income rose 6% from the prior-year quarter to $662M, but was short of the consensus of $675.60M. NII fell 3% on a quarterly basis.

Net interest margin rose to 3.27% from the previous year's 3.10%, but remained below the prior quarter's 3.31%. First-quarter NIM fell short of the consensus estimate of 3.31%.

During the first quarter, the company reported a negative $7M  provision for credit losses, compared with a positive $18M during the same period a year ago. The average analyst estimate for the first quarter loan losses came in at $23.28M.

Total deposits stood at $76.9B during the quarter, up from $75.85B estimates. Loans and leases came at $61.3M, up from $60.9M in the prior quarter and $59.9M a year ago. The S&P Cap IQ estimate for Q1 loans stood at $61.2M.

The estimated CET1 capital ratio rose to 11.5% at the end of March from 10.8% at the end of December 2025.

The Salt Lake City-based bank expects net interest income to grow modestly in the first quarter of 2027 on the back of an earnings asset remix and loan and deposit growth.

data.title: Braskem majority stake sale advances as Novonor signs deal to sell to IG4
data.description:
Braskem (BAK) up 1.7% in Monday's trading as the Brazilian petrochemical ‌company said Novonor and NSP Investimentos signed an agreement to sell control of the company to private equity fund Shine I, which is controlled by asset manager IG4 Capital, Reuters reported.

IG4 confirmed the deal in a separate statement, saying the transfer of shares is ​expected to be completed within the next 30 days, subject to required legal and regulatory approvals.

Also within 30 days, ​Shine I and oil company Petrobras (PBR) are expected to sign a new shareholders' agreement governing ⁠Braskem (BAK).

Petrobras (PBR), which currently owns 47% of Braskem's (BAK) voting shares and 36.1% of its total shares, said it is evaluating the terms of the transaction to make a final decision regarding its waiver rights.

The agreement details the conditions for the court-supervised sale of common shares and Class A preferred shares representing ~50.1% ​of Braskem's (BAK) voting shares and ~34.3% of its total shares, Braskem and IG4 said.

data.title: IT Tech Packaging gets NYSE notice on missed FY2025 10-K filing
data.description:
• IT Tech Packaging (ITP) announced on Monday that it has received a notice from NYSE American for not meeting listing rules due to a delay in filing its FY2025 Form 10-K (due April 15, 2026).
  • The delay happened because the company could not finalize its financial results and complete required disclosures on time without extra time and effort.
  • NYSE American has given the company an initial six-month period to resolve the issue, during which it will continue monitoring compliance.
  • If the company fails to file within this period, it may get an additional six-month extension, but after that, suspension and delisting procedures could begin.
  • The company also said it plans to file the report as soon as possible; however, there is no guarantee of success.
  • The stock closed higher at ~$0.21.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BridgeBio Oncology rises on Fast Track status for pancreatic cancer asset
data.description:
• BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics (BBOT) is up ~8% in after-hours trading Monday after saying the US FDA has granted Fast Track designation to BBO-11818, its candidate for advanced KRAS-mutant pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.
  • The asset is in the phase 1 KONQUER-101 trial and has enrolled patients with locally advanced unresectable or metastatic KRAS-mutant solid tumors.
  • Preliminary data released in January showed a confirmed partial response with BBO-11818.
  • The candidate is an oral "non-covalent inhibitor that targets KRAS in both the ON and OFF states, has high selectivity over HRAS and NRAS, and displays strong activity in KRAS-mutant preclinical models, including KRASG12D and KRASG12V," according to the company.

data.title: Farmers & Merchants Bancorp GAAP EPS of $35.34, revenue of $62.06M
data.description:
• Farmers & Merchants Bancorp press release (FMCB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $35.34.
  • Revenue of $62.06M (+6.7% Y/Y).

data.title: Steel Dynamics GAAP EPS of $2.78 misses by $0.02, revenue of $5.2B beats by $140M
data.description:
• Steel Dynamics press release (STLD): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.78 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $5.2B (+19.0% Y/Y) beats by $140M.
  • Record steel shipments of 3.6 million tons
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $700 million and cash flow from operations of $148 million, which was reduced by the annual companywide retirement profit-sharing distribution of $120 million

data.title: Apple CEO Cook steps down; Ternus named CEO
data.description:
• After nearly two decades leading Apple (AAPL), Tim Cook has stepped down as CEO and will become Executive Chairman. He will be replaced by John Ternus.

This is breaking news...

data.title: FibroBiologics clears Nasdaq compliance, meets $2.5M equity rule
data.description:
• FibroBiologics (FBLG), a clinical-stage biotech company, announced on Monday that it has regained full compliance with Nasdaq listing rules.
  • The company confirmed it now meets key requirements, including minimum $2.5M stockholders’ equity and $1.00 bid price.
  • Although the issues are resolved, the company will still be monitored for one year, and its stock will continue trading normally on Nasdaq.
  • FBLG is trading ~1.31 lower at $1.51 in after-hours trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tanker arrives at Golden Pass LNG terminal to load first export cargo
data.description:
A tanker has arrived at the Golden Pass ​facility in Texas to collect the plant's inaugural export ‌of liquefied natural gas, Golden Pass LNG said Monday, after finally beginning production earlier this year following prolonged delays in construction.

The Al Qaiyyah LNG tanker, which arrived ​at the facility on Monday, is owned by QatarEnergy, which has a 70% stake ⁠in the plant; Exxon Mobil (XOM) holds the other ​30% of the project's equity.

The Exxon-chartered (XOM) LNG carrier HL Sea Eagle ⁠was ​in the Gulf of Mexico on Monday, ​with LSEG ship-tracking data showing it headed to Golden Pass, a sign it could ​load the second shipment from the facility, Reuters reported.

Golden Pass is expected to be one of the largest U.S. LNG export plants when fully operational, with total capacity of more than 18M metric tons/year.

The project has faced delays ​and cost overruns since construction began in 2019, including the bankruptcy of its original lead contractor; Golden Pass said on March 30 that it had produced its first ​LNG.
